item.title: Add --depends-on flag to backlog add and backlog edit
item.intent: Users must be able to declare item dependencies via the CLI — enforcing dependsOn at pick time and check time has no value if users can't set it without editing JSON by hand.
item.description: The dependsOn field exists in the item schema and is enforced by the sprint picker (FEAT-001) and backlog check (FEAT-003), but there is no CLI flag to set it. Users who want to declare that FEAT-002 requires FEAT-001 to be done first must hand-edit the backlog JSON, which is error-prone and undiscoverable. Add --depends-on <ID>[,<ID>...] to both backlog add and backlog edit. The flag should accept a comma-separated list of item IDs and write them to the dependsOn array.
acceptanceCriteria: backlog add --depends-on FEAT-001 creates an item with dependsOn: ["FEAT-001"]
backlog edit <ID> --depends-on FEAT-001,FEAT-002 sets dependsOn to both IDs, replacing any prior value
backlog show <ID> displays the dependsOn list when the field is present
